critical.issue: H10 random SAE baseline undermines Chanin metric validity
critical.description: Random SAE shows 8x higher absorption than trained SAE (0.278 vs 0.034, p < 0.001). This challenges whether the Chanin metric measures learned behavior or structural artifacts.
critical.action: Integrate H10 results into the paper. Report that the Chanin metric detects structural patterns in both trained and random SAEs, and that trained SAEs actually show LESS absorption than random SAEs. Frame this as a methodological finding about metric validity.

critical.issue: Central mechanistic claim is not empirically validated
critical.description: The competitive suppression mechanism is presented as validated but H6 (graph predicts absorption pairs) was falsified with precision@20 = 0.0. The precision-recall asymmetry is consistent with competitive suppression but also with multiple alternative explanations.
critical.action: Reframe the central claim from 'competitive suppression explains absorption' to 'the LCA-SAE correspondence provides a theoretical hypothesis for absorption that remains to be empirically validated.' Add discussion of alternative explanations.

critical.issue: Zero hypotheses survive multiple comparison correction
critical.description: All 12 tests fail Bonferroni correction. H1b (r=-0.431, p=0.028) is prominently featured as 'the strongest signal' but does not survive correction (Bonferroni p=0.334, BH-FDR q=0.107).
critical.action: State prominently in abstract and key results that ZERO hypotheses survive multiple comparison correction. Frame H1b as a non-significant trend, not 'the strongest signal.' Remove claims that 'the inhibition framework explains why delta correction is essential.'

major.description: Perfect r=-1.0 correlation is a mathematical artifact: p_11 + absorption_rate = 1.0 by construction.
major.action: Acknowledge the tautology explicitly in the paper. Remove H9 from any claims about co-occurrence driving absorption. Frame rate-distortion as theoretical speculation, not empirically grounded.

major.description: The paper claims 'exact correspondence' for tied weights but analyzes an untied SAE. The approximation error is unknown.
major.action: Quantify the correlation between W_enc and W_dec^T for the analyzed SAE. Report this as a specific number. Weaken 'exact correspondence' claims to 'approximate correspondence' if correlation is low.

major.description: The claim r=+0.12, p=0.55 for total incoming inhibition vs. absorption rate lacks a verifiable data source.
major.action: Either generate the per-feature graph statistics file or remove specific numbers and use qualitative language.

major.description: The LCA-SAE mathematical correspondence is trivial for tied weights. The 'first connection' claim is about literature synthesis, not a technical contribution.
major.action: Downplay 'first connection' framing. Acknowledge that the mathematical correspondence is straightforward. Emphasize the empirical test (which failed) and methodological framework as the actual contributions.

major.description: The asymmetry is consistent with competitive suppression but also with training dynamics, probe construction, feature splitting, and structural artifacts.
major.action: Add discussion of alternative explanations in Section 5. Test at least one alternative: compare precision-recall on random SAE baseline to see if asymmetry persists without training.

major.issue: Small sample size limits statistical power
major.description: n=26 features with only 4 high-absorption features provides limited power to detect small-to-medium effects.
major.action: Add a power analysis. Report minimum detectable effect size. Avoid strong negative conclusions given limited power.

minor.description: 'Competitive Suppression in Sparse Autoencoders' implies the mechanism is established.
minor.action: Consider a more tentative title: 'Decoder Correlations and Feature Absorption in Sparse Autoencoders: A Null-Result Study' or 'Testing the LCA Hypothesis for SAE Feature Absorption.'

minor.description: A-Z features are orthographic, not semantic hierarchies.
minor.action: Add discussion of how shallow vs. deep hierarchies might differ. Frame results as specific to first-letter features.

minor.description: All primary experiments use GPT-2 Small. Pythia cross-validation was inconclusive.
minor.action: Frame all conclusions as specific to GPT-2 Small. Add model-specific qualifiers to all claims.

minor.description: A claimed contribution (training-free repair) is unvalidated.
minor.action: Either execute the experiment or remove the claim from contributions.

minor.description: Clustering coefficient and std edge weight are not verifiable from available data.
minor.action: Add raw graph data to supplementary materials or note that values are approximate.

minor.description: The LCA dynamics equation appears in both Section 2.3 and Section 3.1 with nearly identical text.
minor.action: In Section 3.1, reference Section 2.3 and focus on implications rather than re-deriving.

optional.description: No experiment manipulates decoder correlations and measures absorption change.
optional.action: Add experiment: orthogonalize subset of decoder directions and measure absorption rate change. This would validate the competitive suppression mechanism.

optional.description: Test whether precision-recall asymmetry persists without training.
optional.action: Run precision-recall decomposition on H10 random SAE baseline. If asymmetry persists, it is a structural artifact, not a learned dynamic.

optional.issue: Pre-register analysis plan for future work
optional.description: Correction methods and primary/secondary hypotheses were not pre-registered.
optional.action: For any future experiments, pre-register the analysis plan including hypothesis families, correction methods, and outlier handling protocols.
